sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Sociedad are in solid form, remaining unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 4 wins and 1 draw, scoring consistently with an average of 2.4 goals per game. Villarreal, despite a mixed bag in their last 5 matches (3 wins, 2 losses), have shown attacking prowess with 8 goals scored, though their defense faltered in recent heavy losses like the 4-0 defeat to Borussia Dortmund.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: The last two encounters between these teams have been competitive, with a 2-2 draw in April 2025 and a narrow 1-0 win for Real Sociedad in January 2025. Matches between them often see goals from both sides, with 4 of the last 5 head-to-head games having no clean sheets for Villarreal.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Villarreal sit 4th in La Liga with 29 points, boasting a strong attack (26 goals scored, 4th in the league) and a tight defense (11 goals conceded, joint-best). Real Sociedad, in 9th with 16 points, have a balanced but less potent attack (17 goals) and a leaky defense (18 conceded), ranking poorly in clean sheets (19th).

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Real Sociedad will miss key forward Mikel Oyarzabal, which could dent their attacking output, though players like Brais Méndez (3 goals, 2 assists) remain threats. Villarreal have no major absences and rely on in-form Gerard Moreno (3 goals in last 5 matches) to lead their charge.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Real Sociedad’s strength lies in creating big chances (6th in the league) and winning tackles (4th), suggesting a high-pressing game, especially at home in Reale Arena. Villarreal, with superior final third entries and dribbling in recent games, may look to exploit Sociedad’s defensive frailties on the counter.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Despite Real Sociedad’s home advantage and unbeaten streak, Villarreal’s superior league standing and attacking consistency give them the edge, aligning with Sofascore’s 54% away win probability. Expect a tight contest, but Villarreal’s form and depth should see them through.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Villarreal leads Real Sociedad 1-0 at the Reale Arena in San Sebastian. Ayoze Pérez struck in the 31st minute with a left-footed finish, giving the away side the advantage. Despite Real Sociedad dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third, they’ve failed to convert their opportunities into goals. Villarreal, on the other hand, made the most of their limited but dangerous attacks, capitalizing on a key moment to take the lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad has shown control in terms of possession and territorial dominance, frequently entering the final third and winning a high percentage of aerial duels. However, their inability to create big chances and convert shots on target into goals has left them trailing. Villarreal, despite being on the back foot for much of the half, demonstrated clinical finishing and solid defensive organization, with fewer final third entries but a higher expected goals tally. Their efficiency in front of goal has been the difference so far, though they’ve struggled to win duels and maintain consistent pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Villarreal, Ayoze Pérez has been the standout performer, not only scoring the crucial goal but also posing a constant threat in attack. His positioning and finishing have been pivotal to the away side’s lead. On the Real Sociedad side, players like Brais Méndez and Takefusa Kubo have shown intent with shots on target, but they’ve lacked the final touch to level the score. Defensively, Villarreal’s backline, including Juan Foyth with multiple clearances, has held firm under pressure, while Real Sociedad’s Jon Martin has been solid with interceptions and tackles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum insights
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Real Sociedad start strongly with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes, pushing forward with intent. Villarreal responded with brief but impactful momentum shifts, particularly around the middle of the half, culminating in their goal. After conceding, Real Sociedad regained control with sustained attacking pressure in the latter stages, while Villarreal’s attacking waves became more sporadic but still carried danger. The home side ended the half on the front foot, hinting at their determination to equalize.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad is likely to come out aggressively in the second half, leveraging their possession and territorial advantage to push for an equalizer. They’ll need to sharpen their finishing and create clearer chances, possibly relying on Kubo or Méndez to unlock Villarreal’s defense. Villarreal, in contrast, may focus on defending their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities, with Pérez remaining a key threat. The away side’s discipline will be tested as Real Sociedad ramps up the pressure, but their clinical edge could see them extend the lead if given space.
